wiki_text: Folk music and fiddling
viki_text: Someone who plays it is a “fiddler”.

wiki_text: In this position the first finger is on a "low first position" note, e.g. B on the A string, and the fourth finger is in a downward extension from its regular position, e.g. D on the A string, with the other two fingers placed in between as required.
viki_text: This means that finger 1 (the fingers are numbered from 1 to 4, the thumb being behind the neck of the instrument) is playing a note which is a whole tone above the open string, e.g. on the D string it is playing a stopped E. When he or she is more advanced they will play in other positions by moving the left hand up the fingerboard nearer to the bridge.

wiki_text: Some cheaper bows use synthetic fiber.
viki_text: Some players today use bows made of carbon fibre.

wiki_text: The violin typically has four strings, usually tuned in perfect fifths with notes G3, D4, A4, E5, and is most commonly played by drawing a bow across its strings, though it can also be played by plucking the strings with the fingers (pizzicato) and by striking the strings with the wooden side of the bow (col legno). Violins are important instruments in a wide variety of musical genres.
viki_text: The violin is a string instrument that is played with a bow. The violin has four strings, which are tuned to the notes, G, D, A, and E.

wiki_text: Tuning Violins are tuned by turning the pegs in the pegbox under the scroll, or by adjusting the fine tuner screws at the tailpiece. All violins have pegs; fine tuners (also called fine adjusters) are optional.
viki_text: To make it easier to tune the violin, many people find it helpful to have “adjusters” for “fine tuning” when the string is only slightly out of tune.

wiki_text: Violins also appear in ensembles supplying orchestral backgrounds to many jazz recordings.
viki_text: In recent years the violin has also been used in jazz playing.

wiki_text: When played as a folk instrument, the violin is usually referred to in English as a fiddle (although the term fiddle can be used informally no matter what the genre of music).
viki_text: This word can be used as a nickname for the violin, but is properly used when talking about folk music.

wiki_text: Crème brûlée (; ), also known as burnt cream or Trinity cream, is a dessert consisting of a rich custard base topped with a texturally contrasting layer of hardened caramelized sugar.
viki_text: Crème brûlée Crème brûlée is a rich custard dish with a crunchy, caramel crust.

wiki_text: The earliest known recipe for crème brûlée appears in François Massialot's 1691 cookbook Cuisinier royal et bourgeois.
viki_text: The recipe was first published in François Massialot's 1691 cookbook.

wiki_text: Walruses prefer shallow shelf regions and forage primarily on the sea floor, often from sea ice platforms.
viki_text: Walruses live mostly in shallow waters above the continental shelves.

wiki_text: Pointe technique is the part of classical ballet technique that concerns pointe work, in which a ballet dancer supports all body weight on the tips of fully extended feet within pointe shoes.
viki_text: Pointe technique is a ballet term describing steps performed en pointe, or on the tips of the toes.

wiki_text: Pointe technique resulted from a desire for female dancers to appear weightless and sylph-like.
viki_text: Pointe technique was developed in the early and middle 19th century to give the ballerina the appearance of being lighter than air.

wiki_text: Buddhism was introduced to Bhutan in the 7th century AD.
viki_text: Bhutan came in the 7th century AD in the Buddhist sphere of influence from Tibet.

wiki_text: Tibetan king Songtsän Gampo (reigned 627-649), a convert to Buddhism, ordered the construction of two Buddhist temples, at Bumthang in central Bhutan and at Kyichu Lhakhang (near Paro) in the Paro Valley.
viki_text: Legend has it, that the Tibetan king and conqueror Songtsen Gampo was opposed by a huge female demon, which had either themselves over a large area. The King decided on every major joint of the demon to build a Buddhist temple, including two in Bhutan: the Kyichu Lhakhang in Paro Valley and the Jampey Lhakhang in Bumthang Valley.

wiki_text: Most species of moth are nocturnal, but there are also crepuscular and diurnal species.
viki_text: Most moths live at night, but some live during the day.

wiki_text: After the fall of the Western Roman Empire, the town was conquered by the Heruli and the Ostrogoths, recaptured by the Romans, but then conquered again by the Lombards and then the Franks of Charlemagne (773).
viki_text: After the fall of the Roman empire, it passed under the control of the Ostrogoths, the Lombards and the Franks.

wiki_text: In 1706, during the Battle of Turin, the French besieged the city for 117 days without conquering it.
viki_text: In 1706, French troops besieged Turin: the siege was interrupted by the arrival of the Austrian soldiers.

wiki_text: In 1861, Turin became the capital of the newly proclaimed united Kingdom of Italy until 1865, when the capital was moved to Florence, and then to Rome after the 1870 conquest of the Papal States.
viki_text: Thus, in 1861, Turin became the first capital of the Kingdom of Italy (which lasted until 1865).

wiki_text: In 1920 the Lingotto Fiat factory was occupied.
viki_text: Lingotto, the headquarters of Fiat

wiki_text: In the 1st century BC (probably 28 BC), the Romans founded Augusta Taurinorum.
viki_text: It became a Roman city in 28 B.C. under the name of Augusta Taurinorum.

wiki_text: It is the capital city of Piedmont and of the Metropolitan City of Turin, and was the first Italian capital from 1861 to 1865.
viki_text: Turin is the capital of the Piedmont region and the capital of the province.

wiki_text: The city serves as the headquarters to Fiat (Fabbrica Italiana Automobili Torino; Turin Italian Automobiles Factory), which has since been absorbed by its parent company, the Fiat Chrysler Automobiles group in London, the seventh-largest automotive company in the world.
viki_text: Fiat (which means Turin Italian Car Factory): it is the main Italian car manufacturing business.

wiki_text: The city used to be a major European political centre. From 1563, it was the capital of the Duchy of Savoy, then of the Kingdom of Sardinia ruled by the Royal House of Savoy, and the first capital of the unified Italy (the Kingdom of Italy) from 1861 to 1865.
viki_text: Turin was particularly important in the history of the Italy: It was indeed the capital of the Savoy duchy and was the capital of Italy from 1861 to 1865.

wiki_text: Dexterity and coordination games This class of games includes any game in which the skill element involved relates to manual dexterity or hand-eye coordination, but excludes the class of video games (see below).
viki_text: Coordination game - games that involve skill and hand-eye coordination

wiki_text: Some collectible card games such as are played with a small selection of cards that have been collected or purchased individually from large available sets.
viki_text: Card game - played using playing cards or sometimes special cards made for the game

wiki_text: The term "game" in this context may mean either a true game played for entertainment, or a competitive activity describable in principle by mathematical game theory.
viki_text: A Game is something people play (often to have fun or enjoy themselves).

wiki_text: Types Games can take a variety of forms, from competitive sports to board games and video games.
viki_text: There are many different types of games, including:

wiki_text: Video games are computer- or microprocessor-controlled games.
viki_text: Video game - played on a computer, mobile device, game machine or game console.

wiki_text: Education Luxembourg's education system is trilingual: the first years of primary school are in Luxembourgish, before changing to German; while in secondary school, the language of instruction changes to French.
viki_text: At school, children learn several languages other than Luxembourgish.

wiki_text: In 2011, according to the IMF, Luxembourg was the second richest country in the world, with a per capita GDP on a purchasing-power parity (PPP) basis of $80,119. Luxembourg is ranked 13th in The Heritage Foundation's Index of Economic Freedom, 26th in the United Nations Human Development Index, and 4th in the Economist Intelligence Unit's quality of life index.
viki_text: In 2014, Luxembourg was considered the most prosperous country in the world with a GDP of $115,000 per inhabitant.

wiki_text: In addition to the three national languages, English is taught in compulsory schooling and much of the population of Luxembourg can speak English.
viki_text: In primary school, children learn to read and write in German, then later in French, getting English generally as a third foreign language if Luxembourgish is the mother tongue.

wiki_text: Luxembourg ( ; ; ; ), officially the Grand Duchy of Luxembourg, is a landlocked microstate in western Europe.
viki_text: Luxembourg is a small country in Western Europe.

wiki_text: With an area of , it is one of the smallest sovereign states in Europe.
viki_text: It is the second smallest member country in the European Union after Malta.

wiki_text: New Zealand ( ) is a sovereign island country in the southwestern Pacific Ocean.
viki_text: New Zealand is a country in the south-western Pacific Ocean.

wiki_text: New Zealand is a constitutional monarchy with a parliamentary democracy, although its constitution is not codified. Elizabeth II is the Queen of New Zealand and thus the head of state.
viki_text: New Zealand is a monarchy ruled by the British sovereign, representated locally by a governor-general.

wiki_text: The country has two main landmasses-the North Island (Te Ika-a-Māui), and the South Island (Te Waipounamu)-and around 600 smaller islands.
viki_text: It is made up of two large islands (the North Island and South Island) and many much smaller islands.

wiki_text: Medhat Pasha Souq is also a main market in Damascus and was named after Midhat Pasha, the Ottoman governor of Syria who renovated the Souk.
viki_text: Medhat Pasha Souq is also a main market in Damascus and was named after Medhat Pasha.

wiki_text: Since the city has been built up with every passing occupation, it has become almost impossible to excavate all the ruins of Damascus that lie up to below the modern level.
viki_text: Since the city has been built up with every passing job, it has become almost impossible to dig out all the ruins of Damascus that lie up to 2 or 3 meters below the modern level.

wiki_text: The historical role that Damascus played as an important trade center has changed in recent years due to political development in the region as well as the development of modern trade.
viki_text: Damascus used to play an important role as an important trade center. Now it is not because of political development in the region as well as the development of modern trade.

wiki_text: In 1871, after the Franco-Prussian War, the city became German again, until 1918 (end of World War I), when it reverted to France. After the defeat of France in 1940 (World War II), Strasbourg came under German control again; since the end of 1944, it is again a French city.
viki_text: During the last two centuries, Strasbourg was French up to 1871, then German from 1871 to 1919, and finally French since 1919, except the 1939-1945 period.

wiki_text: Strasbourg is situated at the eastern border of France with Germany.
viki_text: Strasbourg is a city located in north-eastern France, next to the Rhine river which forms the German border.

wiki_text: Giraffes usually inhabit savannahs and open woodlands.
viki_text: Giraffes usually live in the savannahs, on prairies and open woodlands of Africa.

wiki_text: Giraffes usually inhabit savannahs and woodlands.
viki_text: Giraffes are commonly found on savannahs and prairies or in woods.

wiki_text: The giraffe (Giraffa) is an African even-toed ungulate mammal, the tallest living terrestrial animal and the largest ruminant.
viki_text: Giraffe The giraffe is a long-necked, long-legged animal that lives in the grasslands of Africa.

wiki_text: Their food source is leaves, fruits and flowers of woody plants, primarily acacia species, which they browse at heights most other herbivores cannot reach.
viki_text: Their main food is Acacia leaves.

wiki_text: They prefer Acacieae, Commiphora, Combretum and open Terminalia woodlands over denser environments like Brachystegia woodlands.
viki_text: They prefer Acacia and open woodlands over denser environments.

wiki_text: A teddy bear is a stuffed toy in the form of a bear.
viki_text: A teddy bear is a type of toy that looks like a bear.

wiki_text: While early teddy bears were covered in tawny mohair fur, modern teddy bears are manufactured in a wide variety of commercially available fabrics, most commonly synthetic fur, but also velour, denim, cotton, satin, and canvas.
viki_text: In the early days of teddy bears, they were made from mohair. Now they are usually made from synthetic (fake) fabrics.

wiki_text: Before the advent of Islam, the city was known as (; ). The word Yathrib has been recorded in Surat al-Ahzab of the Quran.
viki_text: In pre-Islamic times the city was known as Yathrib.

wiki_text: Medina is celebrated for containing al-Masjid an-Nabawi and as the city which gave refuge to him and his followers, and so ranks as the second holiest city of Islam, after Mecca.
viki_text: It is the second holiest city of the Islam.

wiki_text: In Rudyard Kipling's 1894 The Jungle Book, the tiger, Shere Khan, is the mortal enemy of the human protagonist, Mowgli. More benign tiger characters include Tigger in A. A. Milne's Winnie-the-Pooh and Hobbes of the comic strip Calvin and Hobbes, both of whom are represented as simply stuffed animals come to life.
viki_text: Famous literary tigers include the one "burning bright" in William Blake's poem "The Tyger"; Shere Khan in The Jungle Books by Rudyard Kipling, the four melting tigers in Little Black Sambo, Tigger in the Winnie the Pooh books by A. A. Milne, and Hobbes in the comic strip Calvin and Hobbes.

wiki_text: Major reasons for population decline include habitat destruction, habitat fragmentation and poaching. This, coupled with the fact that it lives in some of the more densely populated places on Earth, has caused significant conflicts with humans.
viki_text: Its living areas are disappearing due to human developments, and its numbers are declining due to poaching.

wiki_text: On the Indian subcontinent, it mainly inhabits tall grasslands and riverine forests as well as the swamp forests of the Sundarbans.
viki_text: They live in low and highland areas, to mountainous jungles and peat swamp forests.

wiki_text: The Bengal and Siberian tigers are amongst the tallest cats in shoulder height.
viki_text: Siberian tiger (Panthera tigris altaica) is the largest cat in the world.

wiki_text: The tiger (Panthera tigris) is the largest species among the Felidae and classified in the genus Panthera.
viki_text: The tiger (scientific name : Panthera tigris) is a mammal.

wiki_text: The tiger is among the most recognisable and popular of the world's charismatic megafauna. It featured prominently in ancient mythology and folklore and continues to be depicted in modern films and literature, appearing on many flags, coats of arms and as mascots for sporting teams.
viki_text: The tiger has long been featured in literature, especially children's stories.

wiki_text: The tiger is listed as Endangered on the IUCN Red List since 1986.
viki_text: The tiger is an endangered species.

wiki_text: As the tallest building in the world and the first one to exceed 100 floors, the Empire State Building immediately became an icon of the city and of the nation.
viki_text: The Empire State Building is a 103-story skyscraper in New York City.
